svg.text: 16.4 · Money movement — atomic transfer + auth-hold-capture (two-phase)
the simple atomic transfer · and the two-phase hold/capture for card/auth flows (pending vs settled money)
ATOMIC TRANSFER (the common case)
ONE transaction (M07), co-located single-shard (M11/11.9):
debit A · credit B · balanced ledger entries (16.2)
+ idempotency key (16.3) + outbox row (16.12)
atomic conditional UPDATE (no lost update, M08/M15)
= single-shard ACID · all M07–M09 guarantees
money moved exactly once, atomically
AUTH-HOLD-CAPTURE (two-phase, card flows)
① AUTHORIZE → place a HOLD (reserve funds, no move yet)
② CAPTURE → settle (write ledger entries, release hold)
or RELEASE/EXPIRE the hold (funds available again)
available balance = settled − Σ active holds
cross-shard money movement (accounts on different shards) → SAGA (M12/12.8) over local txns + clearing accounts (M11/11.9)
never fragile 2PC/XA · + idempotency + reconciliation · the rare minority (design to co-locate so most transfers are single-shard)
the key subtlety: PENDING vs SETTLED money — held funds aren't double-spent (available = settled − holds); every hold captured OR released (no leaks)
holds have EXPIRATIONS (uncaptured → auto-release) · partial/multiple captures supported · each step atomic + idempotent
💰 every money movement — simple or two-phase, single- or cross-shard — moves money EXACTLY ONCE, atomically, provably
atomic (no loss) + holds (no double-spend of reserved) + two-phase (pending tracked) + idempotent (no double-move on retry)
